rows.title: Livestock types

text_1.eng: Livestock includes domesticated animals raised for food, labor, and other agricultural products

rows.text_1: Livestock includes domesticated animals raised for food, labor, and other agricultural products

rows.title: Livestock keeping

text_1.eng: Livestock keeping, also known as animal husbandry, is the practice of raising animals

text_2.eng:
Livestock kept are:

 * Cattle, Poultry, sheep, goat, 
 * Pigs, Rabbits, and Donkeys

rows.text_1: Livestock keeping, also known as animal husbandry, is the practice of raising animals
rows.text_2:
Livestock kept are:

 * Cattle, Poultry, sheep, goat, 
 * Pigs, Rabbits, and Donkeys

rows.title: Challenges experienced in livestock rearing

text_1.eng: The following are major challenges experienced while rearing livestock

section_1_text.eng:
* Market - You may have milk, but have no market for it
 * Diseases and Parasites - Leads to low production 
 * Technical know-how of feeding and breeding -  Feed is also expensive
 * Ecological conditions (Drought) - Drought- no pasture for livestock, they suffer deficiency symptoms
 * Insecurity and predators
 * Housing challenges

rows.text_1: The following are major challenges experienced while rearing livestock
rows.section_1_text:
* Market - You may have milk, but have no market for it
 * Diseases and Parasites - Leads to low production 
 * Technical know-how of feeding and breeding -  Feed is also expensive
 * Ecological conditions (Drought) - Drought- no pasture for livestock, they suffer deficiency symptoms
 * Insecurity and predators
 * Housing challenges

rows.title: Attempted solutions to livestock production challenges

title.eng: Attempted solutions to livestock production challenges

section_1_text.eng: Value addition, to produce “mala” sour milk from milk

section_2_text.eng:
* Buying improved feeds for specific growth stages
* Monitoring health, like signs of diseases
 * Quarantine
 * Watching out for natural signs of breeding, behaviors, like on heat

section_3_text.eng:
* Planting fodder crops, like bracharia, vetiver grass, and nippier grass
* Reducing the stock/number of animals to that which we can feed comfortably

section_4_text.eng:
* Constructing animal shade
* Proper hygiene and sanitation
 * “Masera/otete,” a portable structure, can carry chicks around
 * Repairing

section_5_text.eng:
* Vaccination before the disease strikes
 * Deworming to control internal parasites
* Sanitation and proper hygiene to prevent    infections, and giving 
 * Seeking/consulting veterinary treatment 
 * Isolating the sick animals 
 * Culling- the sick animals 
 * Handpicking parasites like ticks 
 * Spraying with appropriate cattle dips 
 * Using herbal remedies with available materials like aloe vera, blackjack, and tithonia 
 * Proper feeding

section_6_text.eng:
* Keeping dogs
*Fencing around our homestead
*Installing security lights
*Construction of proper/stronger shelters for livestock 
*Involving local authorities like the chief, “nyumba kumi”

section_7_text.eng:
* Early preparation and stocking of animal feeds, like hay 
 * Digging a well/borehole for water 
 * Supplementing with minerals, like salt lick 
 * Planting fodder crops

rows.title: Factors to consider when preparing feed

title.eng: Factors to consider when preparing feed

section_1_text.eng:
* Breed and species of the animal: poultry,  cow, etc 
 * Age chick, growers, layers, cow, calf 
 * Production lave/purpose -layers or meat 
 * Physiological status - sick, pregnant, male 
 * Sex/gender of the animals.  male animals eat more than females

section_2_title.eng:
Preparing homemade 30kg chick mash concentrates.

 The Ingredients used:

section_2_text.eng:
* Maize (njenga) - 12kg 
 * Maize bran (Ngozi ya mahindi) - 3kg
 * Maize germ (moyo) - 3kg 
 * Wheat/rice bran - 3kg 
 * Soya (njenga) - 4kg 
 * Sunflower white or black (oil extracted) - 2.17 kg 
 * Fishmeal - 1.33 kg 
 * DCP - 0.3 kg 
 * Lime (calcium) - 0.4 kg 
 * Salt - 0.1 kg 
 * Chick premix - 0.3kg 
 * Lycine - 0.1 kg 
 * Methionine -  0.1kg 
 * Toxin binder - 0.2kg

section_2_extra_text.eng:
* Binding toxins means breaking them into smaller components not harmful to 
animals. 
 * Lysine and methionine are among the 9 essential nutrients  
 * Calcium -  can be gotten from egg shells/bones 
 * Ingredients are layered from the heaviest to the lightest

section_4_text.eng:
1. African/ Mexican Merigold can be suspended in the house or used as bedding. 
2. Basil - suspend in the house or spray 
3. can mix tobacco leaves, Mexican marigold, and basil, and burn them inside 
close to the house to eliminate the piece  
4. Lemon grass - dry and put on the bedding of the incubating house. Or plant 
close to the poultry house, even rosemary, to keep them off the poultry house 
5. Rosemary 
6. No bush close to the poultry house / clearing vegetation around homesteads 
7.  Maintain hygiene and sanitation in poultry houses

section_5_text.eng:
* Pawpaw seeds - dry, crush to powder dorm and add the powder in small 
quantities to poultry feeds 
 * Coccidiosis blood in droppings - pawpaw leaves (male or female), crush and 
soak in water, ferment with Molasses for 3 days, and add the mixture to drinking 
water 1:2 (mixture to water), and also, hanging in the poultry house works 
though not effective  
 * Worm wood -dewormer - crush and soak in water for 30-35 minutes

section_5_extra_text.eng:
It controls both internal and external parasites. 
Soak the dried powder in water in the ratio of 1:10 overnight. 
Strain and spray in the poultry houses. For internal parasites, Tephrosia  2ml of Tephrosia solution is given to adults and 1ml for chicks below 8 weeks. For external parasites, the birds are immersed in water twice a week. 
Don't immerse the incubating or laying birds. Instead,  use dry Tephrosia powder/leaves 
and mix them with their beddings.

section_6_title.eng: Concoction for sneezing, coughs, and colds for 10 birds

section_6_text.eng:
Material Ingredients 
 * Ginger - Thumb size 
 * Tumeric - thumbnail  
 * hot pepper 4- 5 
 * Garlic - 2 cloves 
 * Tithonia leaves handful  
 * Aloe Vera  - 2-4

section_6_extra_text.eng:
1.  Soak 30 -45 minutes 
2. For slacking, just add an amount equal to the mixture 
3.  1 tablespoon, 2 liters of drinking water,  or use a syringe 1ml morning 
and evening, or a drop of your finger (undiluted) if the case is severe

text_1.eng: Fermented feeds are feeds that have been made/prepared anaerobically (without oxygen/air) with the help of various microorganisms to improve nutrient absorption and digestibility. We mostly use locally available materials, and the diversity of them ensures a balanced diet, following the go, grow, glow principles 70,25, and 5 percent respectively. Making fermented feed has the following benefits:

section_1_text.eng:
* For improving the gut health of the birds.
 * Reducing the cost of feeding the birds. This is because we use locally available materials, including some weeds, which cuts the cost of buying commercial feeds.
 * The fermentation process is like the beginning of digestion, making the feed easily digestible, and the nutrients are easily absorbed by the body.
 * It introduces probiotics (beneficial bacteria) that boost the immunity of the birds, so they get sick less.
 * Fermenting feed allows for preservation of feed during plenty, to be used later during dry seasons or periods of scarcity
 * Some of the ingredients, like some fodder trees and soya, have anti-nutritional factors that make the nutrients unavailable for absorption. The fermentation process reduces those anti-nutrient factors.

section_2_text.eng: Silage making is preserving animal feeds during times of plenty to be used during scarcity.

section_3_text.eng:
* Fodder materials to be preserved, eg, Neppier
* Maize bran - CHO reduces the moisture content
* Molases
* Chuff cutter/panga
* Airtight container

section_4_text.eng:
1. Cutting Neppier into small pieces about 2cm
2. Put 15cm Neppier, and compact
3. Add bran hands full
4. Sprinkle a mixture of Molases mixed with water in a ratio of 1: 5. Molases
provides lactic acid for the microbes, a source of energy, and helps the
ingredients to compact
5. Compact and cover
6. The procedure until a desired height is reached

section_4_extra_text.eng: Note better: Keep the sillage in airtight conditions, you can use paper/ skyplast/ hematic bags

We mostly use locally available materials, and the diversity of them ensures a balanced diet, following the go, grow, glow principles 70,25, and 5 percent respectively. Making fermented feed has the following benefits:
rows.section_1_text:

 * It introduces probiotics (beneficial bacteria) that boost the immunity of the birds, so they get sick less.
 * Fermenting feed allows for preservation of feed during plenty, to be used later during dry seasons or periods of scarcity
 * Some of the ingredients, like some fodder trees and soya, have anti-nutritional factors that make the nutrients unavailable for absorption. The fermentation process reduces those anti-nutrient factors.
